name: config
context: Validate, inspect, and hot-reload Prometheus config and scrape discovery with promtool.
info: This skill covers the operational lifecycle of `prometheus.yml`: authoring `global`, `scrape_configs`, `rule_files`, and `alerting` sections; validating them with `promtool check config`; resolving discovered targets with `promtool check service-discovery`; inspecting the loaded runtime config; and reloading the server through `/-/reload` when lifecycle endpoints are enabled. Agents use it to ship monitoring changes safely instead of editing config blind.
triggers: check what scrape targets a job resolves to
body:
# Manage Prometheus Config

`prometheus.yml` controls scrape frequency, target discovery, relabeling, rule file loading, remote storage, and Alertmanager delivery. The safest workflow is always the same: validate locally with `promtool`, inspect service discovery if the job is dynamic, then reload the server only after checks pass.

```yaml
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 30s
rule_files:
  - rules/*.yml
alerting:
  alertmanagers:
    - static_configs:
        - targets: ['alertmanager:9093']
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: app
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['app-1:8080', 'app-2:8080']
```

```bash
promtool check config prometheus.yml
promtool check service-discovery prometheus.yml app
curl -X POST "$PROMETHEUS_URL/-/reload"
curl "$PROMETHEUS_URL/api/v1/status/config"
```

The script in this skill wraps the same workflow and adds auth handling through environment variables. Use it before and after every config rollout.

sections.title: Main config shape
sections.body: The main sections agents usually touch are `global`, `rule_files`, `alerting`, and `scrape_configs`. `scrape_configs` is where `job_name`, intervals, discovery, and relabeling live. `rule_files` connects [[prometheus/rules]] into the server, and `alerting` tells Prometheus where Alertmanager is listening.

sections.title: Scrape jobs and relabeling
sections.body: A `scrape_config` defines one job in the common case. Targets can come from `static_configs` or a supported discovery mechanism, and `relabel_configs` can drop, rewrite, or map labels before scrape time. When a job discovers the wrong targets or labels, `promtool check service-discovery` is the shortest path to the truth.

sections.title: Validation and hot reload
sections.body: `promtool check config` validates config syntax and referenced resources, while `promtool check service-discovery` shows discovered targets for a named job. `/-/reload` only works when lifecycle endpoints are enabled. After reload, inspect `/api/v1/status/config` and [[prometheus/query#metadata-and-health]] to make sure the live server matches the intended change.

links.context: Inspect targets and loaded config after a rollout
Rule files referenced by the config should be linted and tested first
Exporter and discovery patterns determine scrape job structure
Retention and remote storage settings live in the main config
Reload endpoints and UI inspection may require auth or TLS

Rule files should validate before being referenced in the main config

scripts.name: Validate and Reload Prometheus Config
scripts.description: Run promtool config checks, inspect discovered targets, and reload or inspect a running server

PROMETHEUS_BASIC_AUTH_USERNAME.description: Basic auth username for protected endpoints

PROMETHEUS_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD.description: Basic auth password for protected endpoints

PROMETHEUS_CA_BUNDLE.description: Custom CA bundle for HTTPS endpoints

PROMETHEUS_INSECURE_SKIP_VERIFY.description: Skip TLS verification for debugging only

PROM_CONFIG_SYNTAX_ONLY.description: Only validate syntax, not referenced files

PROM_CONFIG_LINT.description: promtool lint mode such as all or none

PROM_CONFIG_AGENT_MODE.description: Validate config in Prometheus Agent mode

PROM_SD_TIMEOUT.description: Discovery timeout for promtool check service-discovery

depends.reason: Protected deployments require auth for reload and runtime inspection
Configs that reference rule files should validate those rules as part of the rollout
Discovery and exporter choices shape scrape job configuration
